input_context.reviewer_feedback: REJECT - Review Cycle 1. Issues: 1) Claude JSON parsing failed 2x, 2) Only wails.json read succeeded, 3) No implementation artifacts visible, 4) Spec not provided in context. Corrective: 1) Verify JSON valid before parsing, 2) Read ALL prerequisite files first, 3) Document files created/modified, 4) Note tool failures explicitly, 5) Reference spec fully.
input_context.implementation_notes: Address reviewer feedback. Use custom file_write() syntax instead of Claude JSON to avoid parsing issues. Read detector.go and types.go from internal/terminal/ (not internal/detector/). Create internal/ui/terminal_handlers.go with Wails event emission.

tool_results.content:
# Task Specification: 2.4 - Integrate Terminal Capture with UI **File:** `docs/tasks/2.4-integrate-terminal-capture-with-ui.md` --- ## 1. Task Metadata | Field | Value | |-------|-------| | **Task ID** | 2.4 | | **Title** | Integrate Terminal Capture with UI | | **Phase** | Phase 2: Terminal Integration | | **Estimated Effort** | 4-6 hours | | **Dependencies** | 2.1 (Terminal detection foundation), 2.2 (tmux adapter implementation), 2.3 (Terminal capture service) | | **Section Reference** | Week 7-8: tmux Adapter | --- ## 2. Description This task connects the terminal capture backend (implemented in tasks 2.1-2.3) with the Wails frontend UI. You will update the UI handlers to receive terminal content updates from the capture service and push them to the frontend via Wails events. The terminal preview pane (left column) will display real-time captured terminal content, and users will be able to switch between detected tmux sessions via tab selection. This task matters because it completes the data flow from terminal detection → capture → UI display, enabling the core "Pair Administration" experience where humans can observe AI-managed terminal sessions in real-time. --- ## 3. Inputs ### Existing Files/Artifacts The Coder will need these files (already created by prior tasks): | File Path | Purpose | |-----------|---------| | `internal/terminal/types.go` | Terminal session types and data structures | | `internal/terminal/errors.go` | Terminal-related error definitions | | `internal/terminal/tmux/adapter.go` | tmux adapter implementation | | `internal/terminal/detector.go` | Terminal session detection logic | | `internal/terminal/capture/service.go` | Terminal capture service (from 2.3) | | `internal/ui/handlers.go` | UI event handlers (needs updating) | | `frontend/src/components/TerminalPreview.jsx` | Terminal preview component | | `frontend/src/components/SessionTabs.jsx` | Session tab selector component | | `frontend/src/stores/terminalStore.js` | Frontend state management | ### Existing Interfaces/Contracts ```go // From internal/terminal/types.go type TerminalSession struct { ID string Name string PID int Status string Content string UpdatedAt time.Time } type CaptureEvent struct { SessionID string Content string Timestamp time.Time } ``` ```go // From internal/terminal/capture/service.go type CaptureService interface { Start(ctx context.Context) error Stop() error Subscribe() <-chan CaptureEvent GetSessions() []TerminalSession } ``` --- ## 4. Outputs ### Files to Create or Modify | File Path | Action | Description | |-----------|--------|-------------| | `internal/ui/handlers.go` | Modify | Add terminal event handlers and Wails event emission | | `internal/ui/app.go` | Modify | Initialize capture service and wire up subscriptions | | `frontend/src/stores/terminalStore.js` | Modify | Add terminal content state and update actions | | `frontend/src/components/TerminalPreview.jsx` | Modify | Connect to store and display live content | | `frontend/src/components/SessionTabs.jsx` | Modify | Add session switching functionality | | `internal/ui/events.go` | Create | Define UI event constants for terminal updates | ### Functionality After Completion 1. Terminal content updates flow from capture service → UI handlers → Wails events → Frontend 2. Terminal preview pane displays real-time captured content 3. Session tabs show all detected tmux sessions 4. Clicking a session tab switches the displayed terminal content 5. New sessions are automatically added to the tab list ### New Interfaces/Data Structures ```go // internal/ui/events.go const ( EventTerminalContentUpdate = "terminal:content:update" EventTerminalSessionList = "terminal:session:list" EventTerminalSessionSwitch = "terminal:session:switch" ) // internal/ui/handlers.go type TerminalUIHandler struct { captureService terminal.CaptureService runtime *wails.Runtime currentSession string } ``` ```javascript // frontend/src/stores/terminalStore.js const terminalStore = { sessions: [], currentSessionId: null, sessionContent: {}, // actions: loadSessions, switchSession, updateContent } ``` --- ## 5. Implementation Steps ### Step 1: Create UI Event Constants Create `internal/ui/events.go` with terminal-related Wails event names: ```go package ui const ( EventTerminalContentUpdate = "terminal:content:update" EventTerminalSessionList = "terminal:session:list" EventTerminalSessionSwitch = "terminal:session:switch" ) ``` ### Step 2: Create Terminal UI Handler Struct In `internal/ui/handlers.go`, add the `TerminalUIHandler` struct: ```go type TerminalUIHandler struct { captureService terminal.CaptureService runtime *wails.Runtime currentSession string mu sync.Mutex } func NewTerminalUIHandler(cs terminal.CaptureService, rt *wails.Runtime) *TerminalUIHandler { return &TerminalUIHandler{ captureService: cs, runtime: rt, } } ``` ### Step 3: Implement Session Subscription Method Add method to subscribe to capture events and emit Wails events: ```go func (h *TerminalUIHandler) StartSubscription(ctx context.Context) { events := h.captureService.Subscribe() go func() { for { select { case <-ctx.Done(): return case event := <-events: h.emitContentUpdate(event) } } }() } func (h *TerminalUIHandler) emitContentUpdate(event terminal.CaptureEvent) { h.mu.Lock() defer h.mu.Unlock() payload := map[string]interface{}{ "session_id": event.SessionID, "content": event.Content, "timestamp": event.Timestamp.Unix(), } h.runtime.Events.Emit(EventTerminalContentUpdate, payload) } ``` ### Step 4: Add Session Management Handlers Add frontend-callable methods in `handlers.go`: ```go func (h *TerminalUIHandler) GetSessions() []terminal.TerminalSession { return h.captureService.GetSessions() } func (h *TerminalUIHandler) SwitchSession(sessionID string) error { h.mu.Lock() h.currentSession = sessionID h.mu.Unlock() h.runtime.Events.Emit(EventTerminalSessionSwitch, map[string]interface{}{ "session_id": sessionID, }) return nil } ``` ### Step 5: Wire Up Handler in App Initialization Modify `internal/ui/app.go` to initialize and start the terminal handler: ```go func (a *App) startup(ctx context.Context) { // Existing startup code... // Initialize terminal UI handler a.terminalHandler = ui.NewTerminalUIHandler(a.captureService, a.Runtime) a.terminalHandler.StartSubscription(ctx) // Emit initial session list sessions := a.terminalHandler.GetSessions() a.Runtime.Events.Emit(ui.EventTerminalSessionList, sessions) } ``` ### Step 6: Update Frontend Store Modify `frontend/src/stores/terminalStore.js`: ```javascript import { Events } from '@wailsio/runtime' export const terminalStore = { sessions: [], currentSessionId: null, sessionContent: {}, init() { Events.On('terminal:session:list', (sessions) => { this.sessions = sessions if (sessions.length > 0 && !this.currentSessionId) { this.currentSessionId = sessions[0].ID } }) Events.On('terminal:content:update', (payload) => { this.sessionContent[payload.session_id] = payload.content if (payload.session_id === this.currentSessionId) { this.triggerUpdate() } }) }, async loadSessions() { const sessions = await backend.GetSessions() this.sessions = sessions }, async switchSession(sessionId) { await backend.SwitchSession(sessionId) this.currentSessionId = sessionId }, getCurrentContent() { return this.sessionContent[this.currentSessionId] || '' } } ``` ### Step 7: Connect TerminalPreview Component Modify `frontend/src/components/TerminalPreview.jsx`: ```jsx import { useEffect, useState } from 'react' import { terminalStore } from '../stores/terminalStore' export function TerminalPreview() { const [content, setContent] = useState('') useEffect(() => { const unsubscribe = terminalStore.subscribe(() => { setContent(terminalStore.getCurrentContent()) }) setContent(terminalStore.getCurrentContent()) return unsubscribe }, []) return ( 
{content}
) } ``` ### Step 8: Connect SessionTabs Component Modify `frontend/src/components/SessionTabs.jsx`: ```jsx import { terminalStore } from '../stores/terminalStore' export function SessionTabs() { const [sessions, setSessions] = useState([]) useEffect(() => { const unsubscribe = terminalStore.subscribe(() => { setSessions([...terminalStore.sessions]) }) setSessions([...terminalStore.sessions]) return unsubscribe }, []) const handleTabClick = async (sessionId) => { await terminalStore.switchSession(sessionId) } return (
{sessions.map(session => ( ))}
) } ``` --- ## 6. Verification ### Build Commands ```bash # Build the Go backend cd /home/sblanken/code/paa5 go build -o build/bin/pairadmin ./main.go # Build the frontend cd frontend npm run build # Run the application cd /home/sblanken/code/paa5 wails dev ``` ### Expected Behavior 1. Application starts without errors in terminal 2. Browser window opens with UI visible 3. Terminal preview pane (left column) shows content area 4. Session tabs appear at top if tmux sessions are detected 5. Console shows no JavaScript errors (check DevTools) ### Manual Testing Steps 1. Start a tmux session in a separate terminal: `tmux new -s test-session` 2. Launch PairAdmin: `wails dev` 3. Verify "test-session" appears in session tabs 4. Click the session tab 5. Verify terminal content appears in preview pane 6. Run a command in the tmux session (e.g., `ls -la`) 7. Verify content updates in the preview pane within 2 seconds ### Automated Verification ```bash # Check for compilation errors go build ./internal/ui/... # Run unit tests if available go test ./internal/ui/... # Check frontend build cd frontend && npm run build ``` --- ## 7. Acceptance Criteria - [ ] `internal/ui/events.go` exists with terminal event constants - [ ] `TerminalUIHandler` struct is implemented in `internal/ui/handlers.go` - [ ] Capture service subscription emits Wails events on content updates - [ ] Frontend receives `terminal:content:update` events and updates display - [ ] Session tabs display all detected tmux sessions - [ ] Clicking a session tab switches the displayed terminal content - [ ] New tmux sessions are automatically added to the tab list - [ ] No race conditions in concurrent event handling (verified with `go test -race`) - [ ] Terminal content updates within 2 seconds of terminal activity - [ ] QA_CHECKPOINT_2.4 passes (see `/home/sblanken/code/paa5/QA_CHECKPOINTS.md`) --- ## 8. Constraints & Gotchas ### Known Pitfalls 1. **Race Conditions**: Multiple goroutines accessing session state. Always use mutex locks in `TerminalUIHandler` methods. 2. **Event Flooding**: Terminal content updates can be frequent. Consider debouncing if performance issues arise (max 10 updates/second). 3. **Memory Leaks**: Ensure subscription goroutines exit on context cancellation to prevent leaks. 4. **Wails Runtime**: The `runtime` field must be set after `wails.Run()` initializes. Use `startup()` lifecycle method. ### Platform Considerations - **Linux/macOS**: tmux commands work natively - **Windows**: tmux not available. Handler should gracefully return empty session list (no errors) - **CGO**: No CGO dependencies in this task, but verify `go build` works with `CGO_ENABLED=0` ### Interface Contracts - Do NOT modify `terminal.CaptureService` interface (defined in task 2.3) - Do NOT change Wails event names once defined (frontend depends on them) - Maintain backward compatibility with existing UI handlers - All handler methods exposed to frontend must be thread-safe ### Error Handling - Capture service errors should be logged, not crash the UI - Frontend should show "No sessions detected" when session list is empty - Network/event errors should not break the main application loop --- ## Quality Checklist - [x] Could a competent developer execute this with minimal clarification? - [x] Are all file paths absolute and correct? (`/home/sblanken/code/paa5/...`) - [x] Are function/struct names consistent with existing code? (Follows Go conventions) - [x] Is the verification process unambiguous? (Specific commands and expected outputs) - [x] Does this align with the PRD architecture? (Matches terminal integration phase) --- **Task Specification Complete** *Next Task: 2.5 - Implement terminal command execution interface*
